channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [RELIGARE] Religare Enterprises' Subsidiary Faces Rs. 140.20 Crore Income Tax Demand
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Religare Enterprises Ltd. has received communication from the Office of the Assistant Commissioner of Income-Tax, Central Circle 6(2) Mumbai, stating that Care Health Insurance Limited (CHIL), a material subsidiary of Religare Enterprise Limited, has been issued two Orders under Sections 147 and 143(3) of the Income-tax Act, 1961, for the Assessment Years 2023-24 and 2024-25, respectively. The Orders raise an Income-tax demand of Rs. 140.20 Crores (including interest). However, CHIL believes that the tax demand and applicable interest have been incorrectly computed. The company is in the process of filing a rectification application before the authority, and based on the advice of tax consultants, CHIL would file an appeal against the Order before an appropriate forum within the prescribed timelines. The financials of CHIL are consolidated with REL.

  • Religare Enterprises Ltd. received communication from the Income-tax department regarding a tax demand on their subsidiary, Care Health Insurance Limited (CHIL).
  • The tax demand is for Rs. 140.20 Crores (including interest) for the Assessment Years 2023-24 and 2024-25.
  • CHIL believes the tax demand and interest have been incorrectly computed and is filing a rectification application before the authority.
  • CHIL plans to file an appeal against the Order before an appropriate forum within the prescribed timelines.
  • The financials of CHIL are consolidated with REL.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: RELIGARE ENTERPRISES LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [ARISINFRA] ArisInfra Solutions Ltd Approves Amalgamation of ArisUnitern RE Solutions, Aims to Create a Unified, Scaled Network
item.description:
🔥 Trending

ArisInfra Solutions Limited, a tech-enabled B2B company focused on simplifying procurement of construction materials across India, has approved the amalgamation of its subsidiary, ArisUnitern RE Solutions Private Limited. The amalgamation is set to become effective upon receipt of requisite approvals from the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT), SEBI, the Stock Exchanges, shareholders, creditors, and other regulatory and statutory authorities. The move aims to create a unified, scaled network across services and construction materials, eliminating non-controlling interest and unlocking cross-selling and operational synergies. ArisUnitern has recorded strong and consistent growth since formation, more than tripling its revenues over three years.

  • ArisInfra Solutions Limited has approved the amalgamation of its subsidiary, ArisUnitern RE Solutions Private Limited.
  • The amalgamation aims to create a unified, scaled network across services and construction materials.
  • ArisUnitern has recorded strong and consistent growth since formation, more than tripling its revenues over three years.
  • The amalgamation is expected to eliminate non-controlling interest and unlock cross-selling and operational synergies.
  • The move is part of ArisInfra's strategy to strengthen its position in the value chain and participate more deeply in each project it touches.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ARISINFRA | Category: Joint Ventures

item.title: [IBREALEST] Embassy Developments Ltd Subsidiary Faces Land Resumption Order from KIADB
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Embassy East Business Park Limited, a subsidiary of Embassy Developments Ltd, has received an order from the Chief Executive Officer and Executive Member, Karnataka Industrial Areas Development Board (KIADB), to resume a 78-acre land situated at Kadugodi Industrial Area, Bengaluru Urban District. The order was passed due to alleged breaches of the terms and conditions of a Lease-cum-Sale Agreement executed with KIADB. The company intends to challenge the order, which directs them to surrender possession of the land within 30 days from the date of the order.

  • Embassy East Business Park Limited (EEBP) received an order from KIADB to resume a 78-acre land in Bengaluru.
  • The order was passed due to alleged breaches of the terms and conditions of a Lease-cum-Sale Agreement executed with KIADB.
  • EEBP intends to challenge the order and approach the Hon’ble High Court of Karnataka for suitable relief.
  • The company and EEBP are evaluating the order and its implications.
  • The land is held by EEBP pursuant to a Lease Cum Sale Agreement executed with KIADB, valid up to June 6, 2029.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: INDIABULLS REAL ESTATE LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [CREDITACC] CreditAccess Grameen Ltd Completes USD 40 Million Non-Convertible Bonds Issuance
item.description:
CreditAccess Grameen Ltd has successfully completed the issuance and allotment of 4,000 United States Dollar Denominated Non-Convertible Bonds, each with a face value of USD 10,000. The total issuance amounts to USD 40,000,000. The bonds are secured, rated, and listed, with a tenure of 60 months and a coupon/interest of 240 basis points plus Term SOFR. The interest will be paid on a half-yearly basis, and the principal will be paid as per the terms mentioned in the Information Memorandum. The issue is secured by a charge created by the Issuer in favor of the Trustee, who holds an exclusive first ranking and continuing charge by way of hypothecation over specific loan receivables / book debts.

  • CreditAccess Grameen Ltd completes USD 40 Million Non-Convertible Bonds issuance
  • Bonds are secured, rated, and listed
  • Tenure of the bonds is 60 months
  • Coupon/interest offered is 240 basis points plus Term SOFR
  • Interest payment is half-yearly
  • Principal payment is as per the terms mentioned in the Information Memorandum

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: CreditAccess Grameen Ltd | Category: Investment

item.title: [BHARTIARTL] Bharti Airtel Ltd. Converts Partly Paid-up Equity Shares into Fully Paid-up Shares, Receiving INR 156,959.77 Million
item.description:
Bharti Airtel Ltd. has approved the conversion of 391,176,994 partly paid-up equity shares into fully paid-up equity shares, following the receipt of INR 156,959.77 million towards the First and Final Call. The new fully paid-up shares will rank pari passu with existing shares and will be available for trading on the National Stock Exchange of India Limited and BSE Limited. The Company will also issue reminder notices for the balance 1,110,668 partly paid-up equity shares on which the First and Final Call remains unpaid.

  • Bharti Airtel Ltd. has received INR 156,959.77 million towards the First and Final Call.
  • 391,176,994 partly paid-up equity shares will be converted into fully paid-up equity shares.
  • The new fully paid-up shares will rank pari passu with existing shares and be available for trading on the National Stock Exchange of India Limited and BSE Limited.
  • Reminder notices will be issued for the balance 1,110,668 partly paid-up equity shares on which the First and Final Call remains unpaid.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BHARTI AIRTEL LTD. | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [FORTIS] Fortis Healthcare Ltd. Approves Employees Stock Option Scheme 2026 with Over 96% Vote
item.description:
Fortis Healthcare Ltd. has approved the Fortis Healthcare Limited Employees Stock Option Scheme 2026 with over 96% votes. The approval also includes the grant of employee stock options to the eligible employees of the subsidiary company(ies) and associate company(ies) of the Company under the same scheme. The voting was conducted electronically through e-voting facility and via postal ballot forms. The e-votes were unblocked on March 18, 2026, in the presence of two witnesses. The detailed breakup of voting for each resolution is attached to the Scrutinizer's Report.

  • Fortis Healthcare Ltd. approves Fortis Healthcare Limited Employees Stock Option Scheme 2026 with over 96% votes
  • The approval includes the grant of employee stock options to the eligible employees of the subsidiary company(ies) and associate company(ies) of the Company
  • Voting was conducted electronically through e-voting facility and via postal ballot forms
  • The e-votes were unblocked on March 18, 2026, in the presence of two witnesses
  • Detailed breakup of voting for each resolution is attached to the Scrutinizer's Report

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: FORTIS HEALTHCARE LTD. | Category: Investment

item.title: [PIGL] Power and Instrumentation (Gujarat) Ltd Acquires Additional 8.94% Shares of Peaton Electrical Company Limited
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Power & Instrumentation (Gujarat) Limited has agreed to acquire additional 8.94 % Stake/Shares in Peaton Electrical Company Limited (CIN NO: U31403GJ2006PLC049428) (PECL) which was incorporated on November 22, 2006. The proposed acquisition falls under the purview of the transaction with related parties and the investment has been approved by the Audit Committee and the Board of Directors of the Company. The acquisition is completed by acquiring additional stake 8.94 % increased to 60 % for a cost of Rs. 3.13 Crore. The acquisition will allow the Company and Peaton Electrical Company Limited to collaborate towards product development, new technologies, and preferred relationship for supplies in the area of LT panels, Bus trunking system, Compact Sub stations and other electrical materials business.

  • Power & Instrumentation (Gujarat) Limited has agreed to acquire additional 8.94 % Stake/Shares in Peaton Electrical Company Limited
  • Peaton Electrical Company Limited was incorporated on November 22, 2006 with an object manufacturing unitized substations, M.V. panels, up to 33 KV, LV panels namely motor control centres, power control centres, PLC based panels, Bus trunking system auto synchronizing panels, APFCR panel, and distribution boards
  • The proposed acquisition falls under the purview of the transaction with related parties under Section 177 of the Companies Act, 2013 and Regulation 23 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015
  • The acquisition is completed by acquiring additional stake 8.94 % increased to 60 % for a cost of Rs. 3.13 Crore
  • The acquisition will allow the Company and Peaton Electrical Company Limited to collaborate towards product development, new technologies, and preferred relationship for supplies in the area of LT panels, Bus trunking system, Compact Sub stations and other electrical materials business

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Power and Instrumentation (Gujarat) Ltd | Category: Acquisitions

item.title: [HDFCBANK] HDFC Bank Ltd: Resignation of Part-time Chairman and Independent Director, Atanu Chakraborty
item.description:
🔥 Trending

HDFC Bank Ltd has announced the resignation of Mr. Atanu Chakraborty as a Part-time Chairman and Independent Director, effective immediately. The resignation is due to certain practices within the bank that are not in line with his personal values and ethics. The Reserve Bank of India has approved the appointment of Mr. Keki Mistry as an interim Part-time Chairman for a period of 3 months. The bank appreciates Mr. Chakraborty's contributions during his tenure and wishes him success in his future endeavors.

  • Resignation of Mr. Atanu Chakraborty as Part-time Chairman and Independent Director
  • Appointment of Mr. Keki Mistry as interim Part-time Chairman
  • Resignation due to practices within the bank not aligning with personal values and ethics
  • Approval from Reserve Bank of India for the appointment of interim Chairman
  • Appreciation for Mr. Chakraborty's contributions during his tenure

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: HDFC Bank Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [CEINSYSTECH] Ceinsys Tech Ltd Allots Equity Shares to Allottees Following Warrant Exercise
item.description:
Ceinsys Tech Ltd has allotted equity shares to certain Non-Promoter, Promoter and Promoter Group (Allotees) pursuant to the exercise of share warrants. The allotment is in compliance with the provisions of Chapter V of the SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations 2018. The Allottees include Rare CP Fund I LP, Mr. Sagar Meghe, Mrs. Devika Meghe, and Mr. Raghav Meghe. The total number of equity shares allotted is 30,96,515, with a face value of Rs. 10/- each, at a premium of Rs. 549.90/- per share. The pre and post allotment shareholding of promoter / promoter group has also increased.

  • Allotment of 30,96,515 equity shares to certain Non-Promoter, Promoter and Promoter Group (Allotees)
  • Pursuant to the exercise of share warrants by the allotees
  • Allotment is in compliance with the provisions of Chapter V of the SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations 2018
  • The total number of equity shares allotted is 30,96,515, with a face value of Rs. 10/- each, at a premium of Rs. 549.90/- per share
  • The pre and post allotment shareholding of promoter / promoter group has increased

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ceinsys Tech Ltd | Category: Transmission of Shares

item.title: [KIRLOSIND] Kirloskar Ferrous Industries Limited Announces Merger of Wholly Owned Subsidiaries
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Kirloskar Ferrous Industries Limited (KFIL), a listed material subsidiary of Kirloskar Industries Ltd, has announced a merger with its wholly owned subsidiaries, Oliver Engineering Private Limited (OEPL) and Adicca Energy Solutions Private Limited (AESPL). The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) has passed an order on 17 March 2026, directing certain dispensations and procedures for the merger. The merger is expected to streamline operations and improve efficiency.

  • Kirloskar Ferrous Industries Limited (KFIL) to merge with its wholly owned subsidiaries, Oliver Engineering Private Limited (OEPL) and Adicca Energy Solutions Private Limited (AESPL)
  • The merger is in accordance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015
  • The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) has passed an order on 17 March 2026, directing certain dispensations and procedures for the merger
  • The merger is expected to streamline operations and improve efficiency
  • No consideration is required to be discharged by Kirloskar Ferrous Industries Limited (KFIL) pursuant to the merger

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Kirloskar Industries Ltd | Category: Joint Ventures
